MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LAURANCE MANAGEMENT'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO FILE RESPONSIVE PLEADING.

Appearances via Zoom: Jon James of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415.788.1900) for defendant and moving party; Rahman Popal of the Law Firm for Tenant Rights, Inc. (628.200.3018) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is GRANTED based on attorney fault. Counsel for Defendant admitted to neglecting to respond to Plaintiff in a timely fashion and to attempting to set a hearing on a responsive pleading in a wrong department days after Plaintiff gave warning that a default would be requested and after the default was in fact requested. This course of conduct constitutes attorney fault. Defendant shall file an answer to the operative complaint by 5:00 p.m. on September 5, 2024. Defendant failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. In the future, failure to comply with result in motions being take off calendar or denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
